message:
fix(swarm): log derived lastRotation instead of raw state field in cooldown warning

The cooldown_active log message was printing state.LastRotation (the raw
stored value) instead of lastRotation (the local variable derived after
time zone normalization and validation). This could show a different
timestamp than what was actually used for the elapsed/remaining cooldown
calculation, making the log misleading when debugging rotation timing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -742,7 +742,7 @@ func (r *AccountRotator) OnLimitHit(event LimitHitEvent) (*RotationRecord, error
 		elapsed := time.Since(lastRotation)
 		r.logger().Warn("[AccountRotator] cooldown_active",
 			"session_pane", event.SessionPane,
-			"last_rotation", state.LastRotation,
+			"last_rotation", lastRotation,
 			"elapsed", elapsed,
 			"cooldown", r.CooldownDuration)
 		return nil, fmt.Errorf("cooldown active for pane %s: %v remaining",
